1.name: Salesforce
1.one_liner: A cloud‑based CRM platform that provides REST, SOAP, Bulk, Streaming, and GraphQL APIs for programmatic access to Salesforce data and metadata.
1.access_note: Free developer org signup gives immediate API access; production apps use Connected Apps with OAuth2 and may require security review.
1.api_surface: broad REST, 200+ endpoints (plus SOAP, Bulk, Streaming, GraphQL)

2.name: HubSpot
2.one_liner: HubSpot is a CRM and sales platform that provides APIs for developers to build integrations and access its data
2.access_note: developers can create a private app to obtain an API key or use OAuth for authentication

3.name: Pipedrive
3.one_liner: Pipedrive is a sales CRM that provides RESTful API for integration and automation
3.api_surface: broad REST, with 2 versions (v1 and v2)

4.name: Attio
4.one_liner: Attio is a data-driven CRM platform that enables teams to integrate and automate their workflows with its REST API, App SDK, and MCP server.
4.access_note: requires manual request for developer access, typically takes a couple of days
4.api_surface: broad REST, with webhooks and App SDK

5.name: Twenty
5.one_liner: Twenty is an open-source CRM that provides REST and GraphQL APIs generated from a workspace schema, allowing for customization and integration with other applications.
5.access_note: API keys can be generated in the app's API settings
5.api_surface: broad REST and GraphQL, with CRUD on records and custom objects

6.name: Podio
6.one_liner: Podio is a platform that allows integration with other services and building of tools and applications on top of it through its API.
6.access_note: API key can be obtained from account settings page

7.name: Zoho CRM
7.one_liner: Zoho CRM is a customer relationship management platform that provides APIs for accessing and managing its features.
7.access_note: free developer account and API keys

8.name: Close
8.one_liner: Close is a CRM and sales platform that provides a REST API for integration and automation
8.access_note: API keys and OAuth apps can be created in the Close account settings

9.name: Copper
9.one_liner: Copper is a CRM and sales platform that provides an API for developers to integrate with its services.
9.access_note: developers can register their application and obtain API keys or use OAuth2.0

10.name: DealCloud
10.one_liner: DealCloud provides a comprehensive API for sales and CRM management, enabling developers to access and manipulate data programmatically.
10.access_note: requires system admin role such as Platform Manager
10.main_blocker: requires specific admin role and API key setup

11.name: Zendesk
11.one_liner: Zendesk is a customer service and support platform that provides a range of tools for managing customer interactions and resolving issues.
11.access_note: API tokens and OAuth access tokens can be created by users with valid accounts

12.name: Intercom
12.one_liner: Intercom is a customer messaging platform that allows developers to build integrations and automate actions using its API
12.access_note: free developer account and access token provided after app creation

13.name: Freshdesk
13.one_liner: Freshdesk is a help desk software that provides a range of APIs for accessing and managing support tickets and other data.
13.access_note: API keys can be generated from the Freshdesk account settings

14.name: Front
14.one_liner: Front is a platform that allows developers to build custom integrations and access core data and actions within the app through its API
14.access_note: free developer environment with limited access to 3 teammates

15.name: Pylon
15.one_liner: Pylon provides programmatic access to read and write data within the platform, allowing for automation of business processes and integration with other applications.
15.access_note: requires Admin user to create API token
15.api_surface: broad REST, with various endpoints for accounts, activities, attachments, and more

16.name: LiveAgent
16.one_liner: LiveAgent provides a support and helpdesk platform with API access to conversations, teams, and agents.
16.access_note: API key can be generated by admin in Configuration > System > API
16.api_surface: broad REST, with API v1 and API v3 endpoints

17.name: Plain
17.one_liner: Plain provides Identity and Access Management (IAM) solutions with various APIs for integration
17.api_surface: broad REST and GraphQL, multiple endpoints

18.name: Help Scout
18.one_liner: Help Scout provides a platform for support and helpdesk operations with APIs for custom integrations and automation
18.access_note: API keys can be generated from user profile, OAuth2 application creation is also available

19.name: Gorgias
19.one_liner: Gorgias provides a REST API for reading and writing data in a helpdesk programmatically.
19.access_note: API keys are tied to individual accounts and can be managed in the Gorgias settings

20.name: Gladly
20.one_liner: Gladly is a customer service platform that provides APIs to facilitate seamless customer experiences and integrations with external systems.
20.access_note: API tokens can be generated within the Gladly UI by users with the 'API User' role
20.api_surface: broad REST, with Lookup API and webhooks

21.name: Slack
21.one_liner: Slack is a platform that allows developers to build interactive apps, automate workflows, and integrate with the Slack workspace.
21.api_surface: broad REST, with OAuth 2.0 flow and various tokens

22.name: Twilio
22.one_liner: Twilio provides a range of APIs for communications and messaging, including voice, video, and text messaging, as well as user verification and authentication
22.access_note: free dev sandbox and paid plans available

23.name: Zoho Cliq
23.one_liner: Zoho Cliq is a platform for building internal tools to automate everyday work activities and integrate with other applications
23.access_note: developer documentation and API access available

24.name: Lark (Larksuite)
24.one_liner: Lark is an all-in-one productivity solution that includes chat, scheduling, docs, video conferencing, auto-translation and more in a single platform
24.main_blocker: lack of publicly available API documentation

25.name: Pumble
25.one_liner: Pumble is a messaging and communication platform that allows users to interact with its API for automation and workflow integrations.
25.access_note: API keys can be obtained for integration
25.api_surface: REST, with multiple endpoints and layers of authentication

26.name: Discord
26.one_liner: Discord is a communication platform that enables developers to build applications that utilize authentication and data from the Discord API.
26.access_note: Registration required for developer application and OAuth2 credentials

27.name: Telegram
27.one_liner: Telegram offers a free and open platform for app and website developers to let users sign up and log in with their Telegram accounts, and also provides APIs for further integration and customization
27.access_note: free and open platform, but API Terms of Service apply
27.api_surface: broad REST, with multiple APIs including Bot API, Telegram API, TDLib, and Gateway API

28.name: WhatsApp Business
28.one_liner: WhatsApp Business provides a platform for developers to integrate WhatsApp messaging into their applications and systems
28.access_note: requires Meta developer account, business portfolio, and configured business app
28.main_blocker: requires Meta business portfolio and app configuration

29.name: Aircall
29.one_liner: Aircall provides a public API and webhooks to connect its business phone system with internal systems, CRM, databases, or external tools.
29.access_note: public API with documentation and tutorials available

30.name: Vonage
30.one_liner: Vonage provides APIs for communications and messaging, including Verify API for phone number authentication and Video API for live streaming and video conferencing.
30.access_note: requires sales contact or paid account

31.name: Google Ads
31.one_liner: Google Ads API allows developers to manage Google Ads accounts and create ads to reach goals across all of Google's channels
31.access_note: requires Google Cloud project with enabled Google Ads API and OAuth2 credentials

32.name: Meta Ads
32.one_liner: The Meta Ads API allows users to automate ad creation, management, and reporting across Facebook, Instagram, Messenger, and WhatsApp.
32.access_note: requires a Meta Business Manager account with admin access and an active ad account linked to the Business Manager

33.name: LinkedIn Ads
33.one_liner: LinkedIn Ads is a platform for creating and managing advertisements on LinkedIn, accessible through the LinkedIn API
33.access_note: most permissions require explicit approval from LinkedIn, but Open Permissions are available to all developers without special approval
33.api_surface: broad REST, with multiple endpoints for advertising and user management
33.main_blocker: explicit approval from LinkedIn required for most permissions

34.name: GoHighLevel
34.one_liner: GoHighLevel provides a platform for developers to build and integrate applications and tools using its API, enabling access to its features and data
34.access_note: Developer's Marketplace with OAuth and Private Integrations options

35.name: Mailchimp
35.one_liner: Mailchimp provides programmatic access to its data and functionality through its Marketing API, allowing developers to build custom features to manage audiences, campaigns, and more.
35.access_note: free API key available after creating a Mailchimp account

36.name: Klaviyo
36.one_liner: Klaviyo provides APIs for marketing, ads, email, and social media integration, allowing developers to access and manage data, track events, and implement features such as back in stock notifications.
36.access_note: developers can create an account and obtain API credentials
36.api_surface: broad REST, with multiple endpoints for different features

37.name: systeme.io
37.one_liner: Systeme.io is a funnel builder that exposes a public API for developers to interact with its platform
37.access_note: public API key available in account settings
37.api_surface: REST, with a set of endpoints (e.g. v2)

38.name: Pinterest
38.one_liner: Pinterest API allows developers to manage content, ads, targeting, shopping, and analytics on the platform
38.access_note: requires business account and app setup
38.main_blocker: requirement for OAuth access token and app setup

39.name: Threads
39.one_liner: Threads is a social network where users can share ideas, ask questions, and join conversations, built to help users discover new voices and join communities they care about.

40.name: SendGrid
40.one_liner: SendGrid is an email service that allows users to send and manage emails through its API or web interface.
40.access_note: API keys can be created and managed through the SendGrid dashboard

41.name: Shopify
41.one_liner: Shopify provides APIs for creating ecommerce applications and integrating with online stores
41.access_note: free dev dashboard and CLI for app creation and authentication
41.api_surface: broad REST and GraphQL, multiple endpoints

42.name: WooCommerce
42.one_liner: WooCommerce allows users to create, read, update, and delete data using the WordPress REST API
42.access_note: API keys can be generated in the WooCommerce settings

43.name: BigCommerce
43.one_liner: BigCommerce provides APIs to manage store data, authenticate customers, and build headless ecommerce solutions.
43.access_note: developers can create API accounts and obtain access tokens through the BigCommerce dashboard
43.api_surface: broad REST and GraphQL, with multiple APIs for store data, customer login, and storefront management

44.name: Salesforce Commerce Cloud
44.one_liner: Salesforce Commerce Cloud is an ecommerce platform that provides APIs for custom development and integration.
44.access_note: requires administrator access to create API clients
44.main_blocker: complexity of authentication and authorization setup

45.name: Magento (Adobe Commerce)
45.one_liner: Adobe Commerce provides REST and GraphQL APIs to access products, categories, customers, carts, orders, inventory, and configuration.
45.access_note: create a web services user on Admin
45.api_surface: broad REST and GraphQL APIs, 50+ endpoints

46.name: Squarespace
46.one_liner: Squarespace is an ecommerce platform that provides APIs for third-party integrations and custom development
46.access_note: requires Developer API / Orders API to be explicitly included in the plan

47.name: Ecwid
47.one_liner: Ecwid REST API allows access and management of store data, payment and shipping methods, and storefront settings.
47.access_note: free dev access with a store and custom app

48.name: Gumroad
48.one_liner: Gumroad allows creators to sell digital products and provides an API for integrating with the platform
48.access_note: create an application to generate an access token

49.name: Amazon Selling Partner
49.one_liner: The Amazon Selling Partner API is a REST-based API that helps Amazon sellers and vendors programmatically access their data for orders, shipments, payments, and more.
49.access_note: Requires approval from a selling partner
49.main_blocker: Complex authorization process with multiple token exchanges

50.name: fanbasis
50.one_liner: FanBasis is a payment infrastructure platform that handles payment processing, compliance, and card network relationships for businesses
50.access_note: API documentation available, with login requirement
50.api_surface: REST API, with SDKs in Node, Python, PHP, and Ruby
50.main_blocker: requirement to sign in to access API Docs

51.name: DataForSEO
51.one_liner: DataForSEO provides comprehensive SEO and digital marketing data solutions via API.

52.name: SE Ranking
52.one_liner: SE Ranking provides APIs for pulling large volumes of raw SEO and marketing data, including keywords, backlinks, domain data, and more.
52.access_note: Requires an active SE Ranking account with API access and an API key

53.name: Ahrefs
53.one_liner: Ahrefs is an AI marketing platform powered by big data that helps marketers run search engine optimization, AI search, pay-per-click, content marketing, and more.

54.name: MrScraper
54.one_liner: MrScraper lets you run scrapers through an API endpoint for automation and integration purposes.
54.api_surface: REST, with at least one endpoint for running scrapers

55.name: Apify
55.one_liner: Apify provides a platform for data extraction, scraping, and automation through its REST API and client libraries.
55.access_note: API token can be found on the Integrations page in the Apify Console

56.name: Firecrawl
56.one_liner: Firecrawl gives programmatic access to web data, allowing users to extract content from webpages, crawl websites, and search the web in various formats.
56.access_note: free API key available in the Firecrawl dashboard, also offers keyless access

57.name: Bright Data
57.one_liner: Bright Data provides a developer toolkit for large-scale web search, crawling, and scraping, enabling reliable extraction of pages and structured data without getting blocked.

58.name: Sherlock
58.one_liner: Sherlock allows users to search for the presence of specific usernames across over 400 websites and social networks.
58.main_blocker: no clear documentation on API usage and authentication

59.name: Waterfall.io
59.one_liner: Waterfall.io provides an API for finding and enriching contacts, prospecting by title and company, verifying emails, and more.

60.name: Clay
60.one_liner: Clay is a data enrichment and sales intelligence platform that allows users to connect apps and custom code to create automated workflows and build tools for their team.
60.api_surface: broad REST, with support for HTTP API enrichment and multiple authentication patterns

61.name: GitHub
61.one_liner: GitHub provides a REST API for developers to access and manage their repositories and resources.
61.access_note: free access to REST API with token-based authentication
61.api_surface: broad REST, with OpenAPI compliant documentation

62.name: Vercel
62.one_liner: Vercel provides a comprehensive toolkit for developers to integrate with its API for efficient project and deployment management.
62.access_note: API access tokens can be created and managed inside account settings

63.name: Netlify
63.one_liner: A hosting service for the programmable web, offering a REST-style API for handling website deployments, form submissions, and more.
63.access_note: available as a beta release on Netlify Labs

64.name: Cloudflare
64.one_liner: Cloudflare provides infrastructure and security services for a secure and fast internet.
64.access_note: requires purchase of R2 storage for token generation

66.name: Neo4j
66.one_liner: A graph database platform for storing and querying complex relationships between data

67.name: Snowflake
67.one_liner: A cloud-based data platform for storing and analyzing data

68.name: MongoDB Atlas
68.one_liner: A platform for managing MongoDB clusters and components.

69.name: Datadog
69.one_liner: Provides monitoring and analytics capabilities for developers and organizations.

70.name: Sentry
70.one_liner: A platform for monitoring, error tracking, and performance measurement of applications.
70.access_note: users are supposed to get their own API keys/tokens

72.name: Airtable
72.one_liner: Cloud collaboration and database software with a focus on productivity and project management

73.name: Linear
73.one_liner: A project management and productivity app
73.access_note: free with OAuth2 application creation, or personal API keys

74.name: Jira
74.one_liner: Project management tool for teams to plan, track, and release software

75.name: Asana
75.one_liner: A project management and collaboration platform for teams.

76.name: Monday.com
76.one_liner: A cloud-based work operating system for managing work processes
76.api_surface: REST, numerous endpoints as detailed in the developer docs

77.name: ClickUp
77.one_liner: Use the ClickUp API to build powerful integrations and apps for project management and productivity.

78.name: Coda
78.one_liner: Allows programmatically interacting with Coda docs via API.

79.name: Smartsheet
79.one_liner: Provides programmatic access to Smartsheet's core features for managing sheets, rows, columns, and cells; controlling workspaces and folders; handling attachments and comments; and administering users, groups, and workflows.
79.api_surface: narrow REST, 1+ endpoints (estimated based on documentation)

80.name: Harvest
80.one_liner: A tool for project management and expense tracking.

81.name: Stripe
81.one_liner: Stripe is a payment processing platform that allows users to accept payments online or offline.

82.name: Plaid
82.one_liner: Access and authenticate user banking information for bank account verification, payment initiation, and personal finance management

83.name: Binance
83.one_liner: Official Binance API documentation for various financial products and services.
83.access_note: create API key on API Management page

86.name: QuickBooks
86.one_liner: provides programmatic access to accounting features for small and mid-sized businesses
86.access_note: free dev sandbox likely, requires developer account

87.name: Xero
87.one_liner: Xero's API offers a client credentials grant-type for apps to manage their own app-data via non-tenanted endpoints.

88.name: Brex
88.one_liner: Integrate with Brex's banking services using their public APIs.
88.access_note: free public APIs and no information on restrictions
88.api_surface: broad REST, at least one documented endpoint

89.name: Ramp
89.one_liner: Provides financial and fintech services through an AI-assisted developer API.
89.access_note: requires sales contact, possibly via developer app inside the Ramp app

90.name: PitchBook
90.one_liner: Provides real-time access to company data through its API

91.name: NotebookLM
91.one_liner: NotebookLM is a tool for generating insights using Google's enterprise API.

92.name: Otter AI
92.one_liner: Sales Automatic follow-ups, notes capture, and CRM sync

93.name: Fathom
93.one_liner: Description of the API is not provided, however it involves authenticating all REST API resources with API keys

95.name: Reducto
95.one_liner: A document parsing AI that provides APIs for developers

96.name: Devin
96.one_liner: The Devin API enables enterprise customers to programmatically access detailed usage analytics and manage usage configurations for their teams.

97.name: higgsfield
97.one_liner: unifies access to tools for creating images, videos, voices, and audio from simple inputs like text or static images

98.name: Mermaid CLI
98.one_liner: mermaid CLI has been moved to mermaid-cli, please read its documentation instead

99.name: YouTube Transcript
99.one_liner: Reliable access to YouTube data to retrieve transcript data in real-time for applications.
99.api_surface: broad REST, base URL is https://api.supadata.ai/v1

100.name: Grain
100.one_liner: Grain provides an API for connecting to its services through MindCloud's Universal API
